question_title: How many vehicles will Tesla report producing in 2025?
summary: Tesla has already reported its full-year 2025 production: 1,654,667 vehicles (Model 3/Y: 1,600,767; Other Models: 53,900). This was published in Tesla's Q4 2025 Production, Deliveries & Deployments report on January 2, 2026, and confirmed in the FY 2025 earnings on January 28, 2026. The number represents a 6.7% decline from 2024 production of 1,773,443. Since the resolution data is already public, the forecast is a near-certain point estimate with minimal uncertainty (only risk: restatement).

factors.description: Tesla's official Q4 2025 report (Jan 2, 2026) confirms total 2025 production of 1,654,667 vehicles — this IS the resolution source data
FY 2025 earnings report (Jan 28, 2026) reconfirmed the same production figures
Multiple independent sources (CNBC, BusinessWire, Teslarati) all report the same exact figure
Tiny residual risk of Tesla issuing a restatement or correction to production numbers (historically very rare)

resolution_reason: Tesla has officially reported their full-year 2025 vehicle production figures. According to Tesla's Q4 2025 Production, Deliveries & Deployments report released on January 2, 2026, Tesla produced a total of 1,654,667 vehicles in calendar year 2025. This breaks down as: Model 3/Y: 1,600,767 units and Other Models (Model S, Model X, Cybertruck): 53,900 units. This figure has been confirmed across multiple sources including Tesla's official press release, Business Wire, Teslarati, CNBC, and various financial statistics sites, all reporting the same number. The data comes directly from Tesla's financial reports as required by the resolution criteria.
resolution_criteria:
This question will resolve as the total number of vehicles produced by Tesla in the calendar year 2025, according to Tesla's financial reports.  All street legal automobiles, trucks, vans, and vehicles with 4 or more wheels should be included.

If Tesla does not report this data, Metaculus may use alternative figures from the [OICA](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organisation_Internationale_des_Constructeurs_d%27Automobiles) or another credible source, or resolve as **Ambiguous** at their discretion
fine_print:
***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/6601) which opened on 2021-02-24 23:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print contained above. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
background_info:
In its 2020 Q4 report, [Tesla Motors](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tesla_motors) reported they produced 509,737 vehicles in 2020. They reported 367,500 in their [Q4 2019 report](https://ir.tesla.com/press-release/tesla-q4-2019-vehicle-production-deliveries) and 245,240 vehicles in their [Q4 2018 Report](https://ir.tesla.com/press-release/tesla-q4-2018-vehicle-production-deliveries-also-announcing-2000).

Tesla Motors has [suggested](https://www.cnbc.com/2021/01/27/tesla-tsla-earnings-q4-2020.html) they can maintain a 50% rate of production growth which would suggest they would ship over 3.5 Million vehicles in 2025.
